label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 6

extraction.one_line_summary: VINE Jay attacked from breakaway on final Cat-1 climb in torrential rain, held off EVENEPOEL by 15 seconds while ROGLIC lost 1:37
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL attacked without teammates after Quick-Step domestiques dropped, showing willingness to race exposed in foul weather.
ROGLIC group sat on his wheel unwilling to chase, revealing GC rivals prefer letting him close gaps alone.
YATES attempted to follow VINE's initial move but couldn't sustain, signaling form issues despite Trek defending leader.
PADUAN's 2-minute lead evaporated on descent between Cat-1 climbs due to excessive caution in wet conditions.

rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: bridged solo from peloton to breakaway on final Cat-1, immediately dropped PADUAN despite Ukrainian's earlier gap
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: took 21-second lead despite racing final climb without teammates after PHILIPPE and others dropped
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: lost 1:37 despite entire GC group sitting on his wheel, suggesting either mechanical/tactical choice or genuine weakness
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: matched EVENEPOEL's attack and finished only 1 second back, surprising podium placement indicates improving form
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: lost 2:56 on single stage, catastrophic gap for GC ambitions this early in Grand Tour
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: attempted to follow VINE but quickly abandoned effort, concerning given Trek-Segafredo's leader role
rider_signals.MOLLARD Rudy: neutral: lost leader's jersey but Groupama-FDJ successfully defended it initially before weather/climbs intervened
